Description: The same SSA value name is defined more than once in a function
Typical Root Cause: LLM reuses a virtual register name after reassignment in the source program

Description: An SSA value is used before it is defined or dominates the use
Typical Root Cause: LLM loses data-flow order across blocks or emits incomplete predecessors

Description: `phi` node has malformed incoming pairs or mismatched predecessors
Typical Root Cause: LLM imitates `phi` syntax without matching CFG structure

Description: Branch target label does not exist or no longer matches the block graph
Typical Root Cause: LLM renames labels inconsistently during generation or repair

Description: Basic block lacks a required terminator such as `br` or `ret`
Typical Root Cause: LLM emits a plausible block body but forgets to close the block

Description: Call-site arguments do not match declared callee signature
Typical Root Cause: LLM invents helper calls or drops parameters during repair

Description: Instruction operands do not match expected LLVM IR types
Typical Root Cause: LLM confuses `i1` predicate results with `i32` arithmetic values

Description: LLVM reported an error that does not yet map cleanly to a custom category
Typical Root Cause: Used as a catch-all bucket for future taxonomy expansion

Description: IR passes syntax and verifier checks but returns the wrong observable result
Typical Root Cause: LLM preserves surface structure while changing program meaning

Description: Required LLVM executable is missing from the environment
Typical Root Cause: Local machine is not yet provisioned with the LLVM toolchain
